title: Multipart Upload State, Parts, Checksums, and Cleanup · Runtime Contract: Complete Ambiguous Reconcile
summary: A worker starts multipart uploads without durable state, chooses tiny parts, loses ETags, retries concurrent parts blindly, and never aborts abandoned uploads. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the complete ambiguous reconcile increment in recoverable multipart-upload state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate ordered parts, complete once, and reconcile timeout or connection-loss ambiguity with object metadata and upload state.
Bound part workers and bytes, cancel safely, drain results, preserve completed evidence, and prevent goroutine or buffer leaks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Complete Ambiguous Reconcile · Theory Complete Ambiguous Reconcile
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 7975 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate multipart part state, retained ETag evidence, cancellation, and abort cleanup; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
